description: Length-matched oracle test facts for optimal f(r) combinations in Q_f conservation.

facts.context: For Q_f conservation, using a single f(r) vs an optimized linear combination gives:
distractors: no measurable improvement over any single kernel choice
single-kernel functions are provably the global optimum
roughly 10% improvement with diminishing marginal returns

facts.context: The dominant terms in the optimal f(r) = Sigma a_i phi_i(r) for Q_f conservation are:
facts.truth: e^(-r/2), tanh(r), sin(r) with alternating sign coefficients
distractors: -ln(r) alone with no additional basis function contributions
1/r alone which recovers standard Biot-Savart interactions
r^2 alone corresponding to pure moment of inertia scaling

facts.context: The space of near-conserved Q_f quantities forms:
facts.truth: a vector space where linear combinations preserve conservation
distractors: a discrete isolated set with no continuous interpolation
no algebraic structure connecting different Q_f variants
a nonlinear manifold requiring Riemannian optimization tools

facts.context: Comparing the learned optimal f(r) to the energy kernel f(r) = -ln(r):
facts.truth: optimal is 2700x better (loss 5e-6 vs 1.4e-2 for energy)
distractors: the energy kernel is already optimal and cannot be improved
both perform the same within numerical integration tolerance
optimal performs slightly worse due to overfitting artifacts
